user_outcome: Every dispatch record carries honest economics — real cost computed from the CONFIRMED model's pricing or carried from the CLI's own report, an explicit cost_source, true token split, duration, and confirmed model-id — so verified-slices-per-dollar-per-model is finally a real number (sworn#70), and a subscription dispatch is recorded as subscription-covered, never as fake $0 API spend.
rationale: Closes sworn#70. Grounding against live code: implementer cost is nominal flat $2/1M (internal/agent/agent.go:182 computeCost) and agentic-verifier cost the same (internal/verify/verify.go:246 computeAgenticCost); the S06-operational-readiness pricing surface PriceForModel/ComputeCostFromTokens (internal/model/client.go:72-100) has ZERO call sites (dark code); a SECOND pricing table lives in internal/model/pricing.go (Pricing/ComputeCost) and the two are not cross-wired; Anthropic's correctly computed resp.CostUSD is discarded by the agent loop. After the T4 rewire, cost computation belongs INSIDE drivers (Result.CostUSD/CostSource per the S01 contract): the in-process driver computes from the confirmed response model-id and true token split via the unified pricing registry; subprocess drivers carry the CLI-reported figure. state.Dispatch (internal/state/state.go:83) already has DurationMS/InputTokens/OutputTokens/ModelIDConfirmed — the schema gap is only cost_source; slice-status-v1 is additionalProperties:true so the field is schema-safe (verified against internal/baton/schemas/slice-status-v1.json).
in_scope: Unifying the two pricing surfaces into one registry with one lookup path
Deleting the flat-rate computeCost (agent.go) and computeAgenticCost (verify.go)
In-process driver cost from CONFIRMED model-id + true token split; provider-reported cost carried when present
out_of_scope: Adding pricing DATA for models absent from both legacy tables (fail-closed unknown instead)
Cross-run durable telemetry storage (FT-7 internal/db work)

effort_complexity.rationale: Mechanical but wide: unify two pricing tables, delete two flat-rate functions, add cost_source through driver -> Result -> Dispatch -> status.json, tests at each layer.

risks.description: Pricing-table unification silently changes recorded costs for models present in both tables with different rates.
risks.mitigation: The unification test enumerates every model present in either table and asserts the unified rate equals the per-driver-map rate (client.go's maps win — they are per-provider-confirmed); divergences are listed in the slice journal, not silently absorbed.

risks.description: cost_source addition breaks status.json consumers or fixtures in other packages (the strict-reader cross-package lesson).
risks.mitigation: Field is additive and omitempty; slice-status-v1 is additionalProperties:true; the track merge gate runs full `go test ./...`.

acceptance_criteria.text: The two pricing surfaces (internal/model/pricing.go Pricing/ComputeCost and internal/model/client.go PriceForModel/ComputeCostFromTokens) SHALL be unified into one registry with one lookup path; the flat-rate computeCost (internal/agent/agent.go:182) and computeAgenticCost (internal/verify/verify.go:246) SHALL be deleted; TestPricingUnified asserts every model from either legacy table resolves to exactly one rate.

When an in-process driver dispatch completes, Result.CostUSD SHALL be computed from the CONFIRMED response model-id (not the requested string) and the true input/output token split via the unified pricing registry, with CostSource="pricing-table"; when the provider itself reports a cost (Anthropic resp.CostUSD), that figure SHALL be carried with CostSource="provider" instead of recomputed.

When a subprocess CLI dispatch reports its own cost (claude-cli total_cost_usd), Result SHALL carry it with CostSource="cli"; when a subscription-covered CLI reports 0 by design, the record SHALL show CostUSD=0 with CostSource="subscription" — never a fabricated API-equivalent price.

If no pricing entry exists for a confirmed model-id, CostUSD SHALL be recorded as 0 with CostSource="unknown" (fail-closed honesty) and a warning logged naming the model — never a guessed or defaulted rate.

state.Dispatch SHALL persist cost_source (additive, omitempty) alongside the existing DurationMS/InputTokens/OutputTokens/ModelIDConfirmed fields; a written status.json containing dispatches SHALL validate against slice-status-v1; `go test ./internal/model/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/state/... ./internal/run/...` SHALL pass.
